description:
You are acting as a Kubernetes platform engineer who has just received a **pre-seeded SSH key-pair** that must be
  1) installed for your interactive Linux user,
  2) exposed to a Git-pulling controller running inside the cluster via a Kubernetes Secret, and
  3) fully documented in a short log file.

  The automated grader will look for the *exact* files, paths, formats and key names mentioned below, so please follow them to the letter.

  Goal summary
  ------------
  A.  Install the key-pair that already exists in
        /home/user/preseed/id_ed25519
        /home/user/preseed/id_ed25519.pub
     into the normal OpenSSH locations under /home/user/.ssh/

  B.  Make the public key usable for inbound SSH by adding it to
        /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys
     (append; do NOT delete anything that might already be there).

  C.  Create a Kubernetes manifest directory
        /home/user/k8s/operator
     and, inside it, a Secret manifest file named
        secret-ssh-key.yaml
     that Base64-encodes the *private* key and places it under the data.id_ed25519
     field in the manifest shown below.

  D.  Produce a human- and machine-readable log file
        /home/user/ssh_key_setup.log
     whose contents are precisely formatted as **seven single-line KEY=VALUE pairs**
     in the exact order shown beneath.  The grader will parse this log file byte-for-byte,
     so do not add extra spaces, additional quotation marks, blank lines, or colour codes.

     Required log format (each item on its own line, terminated by a single newline):
       TIMESTAMP=<ISO-8601 date-time, e.g. 2025-02-16T14:22:31Z>
       PRIVATE_KEY_PATH=/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519
       PRIVATE_KEY_MD5=<32-char hex lowercase md5sum of /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519>
       PUBLIC_KEY_PATH=/home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
       AUTHORIZED_KEYS=/home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys
       K8S_SECRET_MANIFEST=/home/user/k8s/operator/secret-ssh-key.yaml
       STATUS=COMPLETED

     Notes on the log:
       • The TIMESTAMP must be in UTC and **must use the letter ‘Z’** to mark Z-time.  
       • The md5 hash must match the actual key file that ends up in ~/.ssh.  
       • STATUS must literally be the word “COMPLETED” (upper-case).

  E.  File permissions must satisfy OpenSSH security rules:
       ~/.ssh               → 700
       ~/.ssh/id_ed25519    → 600
       ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub→ 644
       ~/.ssh/authorized_keys → 600  (create if missing, else preserve other keys)

  F.  The Secret manifest **must** exactly match the YAML skeleton shown here (indent with two spaces):
       apiVersion: v1
       kind: Secret
       metadata:
         name: git-ssh-key
         namespace: operators
       type: Opaque
       data:
         id_ed25519: <BASE64-OF-PRIVATE-KEY>

     • Replace <BASE64-OF-PRIVATE-KEY> with the base64 encoding of the full contents
       of /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519 (including its trailing newline, if any).  
     • Do not wrap the base64 string; keep it on a single logical line.  
     • The file must end with exactly one newline character.

  What the grader will check
  --------------------------
  1.  Existence and permissions of /home/user/.ssh/ and its files.  
  2.  The presence of the exact public-key line from id_ed25519.pub inside
      /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys (verbatim, including trailing newline).  
  3.  That /home/user/k8s/operator/secret-ssh-key.yaml is valid YAML and that the
      value of data.id_ed25519 is the correct base64 of the private key.  
  4.  That /home/user/ssh_key_setup.log exists, follows the required seven-line
      format exactly, and that  
        • PRIVATE_KEY_MD5 actually matches `md5sum /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519`, and  
        • STATUS equals “COMPLETED”.  

  You do NOT have to create the pre-seed files; they already exist.  
  You must not use sudo or change ownership of any other system paths.  
  Accomplish the goal with normal shell utilities that are available in a
  standard Ubuntu-like environment.

  When you are finished, ensure that:
    • All paths, file names and contents are *exactly* as specified.  
    • The log file contains the correct md5 sum and a valid UTC timestamp.  
    • No additional text is output outside of normal terminal command responses.  

  Good luck—pay close attention to every required detail!
truth:
<!--
    Pre-seeded artefacts (already present when the agent begins):

    1. Private key file
       Path   : /home/user/preseed/id_ed25519
       Mode   : 0644
       Content (including trailing newline):
         myprivatekey⏎     <-- The ⏎ symbol represents a literal newline (0x0a)

    2. Public key file
       Path   : /home/user/preseed/id_ed25519.pub
       Mode   : 0644
       Content (including trailing newline):
         ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIG15cHVibGlja2V5 user@training⏎

    3. Base64 representation that MUST appear in the Secret manifest:
       bXlwcml2YXRla2V5Cg==

    4. The exact public-key line that must be present in ~/.ssh/authorized_keys:
       ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIG15cHVibGlja2V5 user@training⏎

    5. After correct completion, the following new artefacts will exist:

       a) /home/user/.ssh/ (700) with:
            id_ed25519      (600, same content as #1)
            id_ed25519.pub  (644, same content as #2)
            authorized_keys (600, containing at least the line shown in #4)

       b) /home/user/k8s/operator/secret-ssh-key.yaml  containing the YAML
          snippet shown in the public task, with data.id_ed25519 set to
          “bXlwcml2YXRla2V5Cg==” (and exactly one terminating newline).

       c) /home/user/ssh_key_setup.log having *exactly* 7 lines,
          last line “STATUS=COMPLETED⏎”.

    6. The grader will compute `md5sum /home/user/.ssh/id_ed25519` at runtime,
       then verify that the same 32-char hex value appears on the
       PRIVATE_KEY_MD5= line inside the log file, so the actual hash value does
       not need to be hard-coded here.
  -->
name: task_000000_5178d722
